events.title: Screen Time with Toronto Public Health
events.description: In this session, the benefits and impacts of screen time will be explored. Discussion will include looking at recommendations for screen time per age of the child (between 0 and 6 years of age) and how to model and manage appropriate screen time behaviour. Physical activity recommendations as an alternative to screen time will be reviewed.

events.title: Fay and Fluffy's Storytime
events.description: Join Fay & Fluffy (stars of Family Jr's The Fabulous Show with Fay & Fluffy) as they share some of their favourite books and stories. events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

events.description: In honour of Asian Heritage Month, Bendale presents​ a performance of Bharatanatyam, the oldest dance tradition of India. Originating in the Southern part of India, the performance will highlight beautiful music, costume and story telling using hand gestures and expressions.  Drop-in All Ages. The program will take place in the Auditorium located in the basement. Elevator on site. If you have any questions, please call Bendale Branch at 416-396-8910.

A brief history of kimbap, its differences from the Japanese sushi roll, and eating etiquette will also be shared. Prizes are also awarded for best kimbap creations. events.title: Mimico Kids & Parents Book Club
events.description: Our book for May is Grimm's Fairy Tales by Jakob & Wilhelm Grimm. First published in 1812, these timeless stories have enchanted generations of children. Some of the stories are scary, some whimsical, but all are spellbinding and have had a huge influence down through the years. These stories first introduced characters such as Hansel and Gretel, Rapunzel, Tom Thumb, and many more. Copies available at the library. Grimm's Fairy Tales

events.title: Sew Cool Kids:  Cross-Stitch
events.description: This fun and creative 4 week program introduces children aged 6 - 12 to the art of cross-stitching! Kids will learn the basic stitches, how to follow simple patterns, and how to use colorful threads to bring a design to life.  By the end of the program, each child will have completed a bright and cheerful stitched picture to proudly take home and display. Registering will register your child for all 4 sessions.

events.title: Instructional Chess

Children under 12 need to be supervised.

events.title: "Jane and the Blue Willow Princess" with Author Catherine Little
events.description: On a beautiful spring day, sisters Jane and Cassandra enjoy tea with Mother in the garden. Jane is in the throes of writing a story for Father’s birthday, but too many ideas swirl in her head, and she cannot get started. The Blue Willow pattern on the tea set catches Jane’s attention … will that be the inspiration she’s looking for?​ Catherine Little presents a reading from her picture book " Jane and the Blue Willow Princess " about author Jane Austen, then leads a fun craft with air-dry clay.&

events.title: Making Comics Camp with Kean Soo
events.description: Learn how to write and draw comics & graphic novels with Kean Soo, the award-winning creator of Jellaby and March Grand Prix!  Kean will lead you through the basics of creating fun, compelling characters, story outlines, and then he will show you how to combine everything together to lay out and draw your own comic!  Program runs for three days July 20th to July 22nd, from 1pm-3pm. Each child must be available to attend all three days. Program is suitable for ages 9 to 11 yea

events.title: Retro Movie: Babe
events.description: Nominated for six Academy Awards, this movie follows the life of the pig Babe, and his talents at shepherding. Rated G. Ages 5-12. Drop-in.

events.title: Pottery Camp with Children's Art Studio
events.description: In this fun, hands-on pottery camp, young artists will explore clay sculpture while building confidence and creative thinking in a supportive, playful environment. Participants will learn a variety of hand-building techniques, including shaping, sizing, coiling, and adding creative surface details. Presented by the Children's Art Studio. For children ages 8-10 years. Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Public Library under the Public L

events.title: STEM Camp with Relay Education
events.description: Join with Relay Education to experience dynamic, hands-on workshops with exciting demonstrations and interactive experiments using unique wind, solar, and biogas models!  The camp runs for 3 days on July 21, 22 and 23 from 1-3 pm.  For children ages 8-12. Space is limited. Registration begins July 6, in branch or calling 416-396-3599.

events.title: Hands-on K'NEX 3-D Construction & Destruction Challenge
events.description: Have fun and collaborate to imagine, design, and construct lightweight, high‑strength 3‑D bridges using supplied K’NEX materials. Then evaluate the structures through controlled load testing using a specialized “Bridge‑Crusher” rig, which reveals each bridge’s performance through its strength‑to‑weight ratio. events.title: Anne of the Library-on-the-Hill
events.description: 'Growing up in the shadow of the Great War, Anne finds comfort in her neighbourhood library, where she loses herself in books, often imagining herself as part of the story. events.title: How to Write Non-fiction
events.description: Writing nonfiction can be a challenge but author Erin Silver will make it fun and easy! We will discuss what nonfiction writing is, how we can make it sound more like fiction, and how to organize and write engaging A+ nonfiction! Erin will share insider tips about her process, where her ideas come from, and will also read from her nonfiction book, Rush Hour: Navigating Our Global Traffic Jam .

events.description: The North York Central Library and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Activities change each week, so there’s always something new to explore!

events.title: Indigenous Film Screening - Nechako: It Will Be a Big River Again
events.description: Nechako is a crucial documentary from Lyana Patrick that follows two Indigenous Nations fighting for our collective future. When the Kenney Dam was built in the 1950s, it diverted 70 percent of the Nechako River into an artificial reservoir, severely impacting the lives of local Stellat’en and Saik’uz Nations. What followed were decades of resistance, including legal actions against the Canadian federal and provincial governments and Rio Tinto Alcan, a subsidiary of a global mining conglomerate.

events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? events.title: POV: You Found a Good Book
events.description: Not your average book club. Think less assigned reading, more good vibes, real talk, and stories that actually hit. No pressure, no quizzes—just snacks, chill convos, and books you might actually want to finish. Come hang out, meet people your age, and help pick what we read next. You might even find a book you don’t hate.  Drop in. Bring a friend. Or just bring yourself—we saved you a spot. The Hate U Give

events.title: Guildwood Chess Club
events.description: Join the Tuesday chess club at Guildwood Library , as we play chess every Tuesday starting on May 5 th . Players of all strengths and skill level are welcomed to participate in the club. Chess introduction lessons are available for new players that are new to the game. The program is for children ages 7+, teens, and adults . For More Information, please call the Guildwood Library at 416-396-8872. Drop-In. No registration required. events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Games
events.description: Drop in to the Youth Hub every Tuesday and Thursday to play video games. Play with friends, by yourself, or maybe someone new.  Tuesdays will be Nintendo Switch days and Thursdays will be PS4 days.  *NEW* Starting at 3:30 pm, teens can sign up for a time slot to use the consoles. If there is no demand, gaming will be awarded on a first come first serve basis.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, wher

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Youth Hub:  Nail Night
events.description: Unleash your creativity as you design your own stunning nail art! Join us for one or all of our fabulous Nail Nights on the first Monday of every month. Drop-in, no registration required. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: LivingWorks safeTALK
events.description: LivingWorks safeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. Learn practical skills to help someone keep safe and guide them toward further help.  Who should attend? Individuals aged 15 and up People who want to support suicide safety in their community No previous training or experience is required. All participants will receive a LivingWorks safeTALK certificat

events.title: Retirement Planning & Living
events.description: It's never too late to start thinking about investing! Managing your finances to maximize your retirement years is the goal of retirement planning. Your retirement strategy should strike a balance between your financial realities, requirements and wants. This presentation will cover how to plan for retirement, sources of retirement income and how to protect yourself from scams, frauds and financial abuse Presented by Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization (CIRO) , a national self-regulatory

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around Taylor Memorial
events.description: Taylor Memorial Branch and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Registration is required   The tour will start and end at the Taylor Memorial meeting room and will be guided by the City of Toronto U

events.title: Pawsitively Pets: Hands-on Animal Education
events.description: Pawsitively Pets is a hands-on animal education program for children of all ages. Our wonderful animal team is made up of an assortment of rescued pets that have found their forever home with us. Our goal is to provide our animal team with a permanent home and the best possible care, while sharing our love or animals with others. events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.

events.title: Retirement Planning
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop is an introduction to long-term financial planning including retirement savings and better understanding of federal income benefits, such as, Canada Pension Plan (CPP) and Old Age Security (OAS). Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. This year we are reading:  February 19 -  The Strangers by Katherena Vermette April 16 -  Big Little Lies by Liane Moriarty June 18 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 17 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 19 -  Small Things Like These by
